name: catnip
effects: traditional-use records describe calming and digestive-soothing applications
interactions: Potential additive sedation with sedative medications or alcohol is plausible but not well quantified.
safetyNotes: Human safety data are limited and product composition can vary substantially by preparation.
description: Catnip (Nepeta cataria) is a Nepeta genus herb used in traditional preparations, with modern literature emphasizing phytochemistry and preclinical pharmacology.
dosage: No standardized evidence-based adult therapeutic dosing was established in the promoted source.
duration: No direct duration data. Contextual inference: Used in folk medicine for relaxation, colds, and digestive issues... No direct mechanismofaction data. preparation: Most common forms are dried-leaf infusions and tinctures with variable constituent strength.
region: Native to parts of Europe and Asia and now naturalized widely, including North America.
traditionalUse: Traditional preparations include tea use for restlessness, digestive discomfort, and mild cold-related symptoms.
